message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-20_21-58-27_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "3-year customer's 2nd contract, cursed sword (steel, enchantment 7): 100 + 50 + 30 − 20 + 10 − 15 + 5 = 160 G"
Current error: expected 135 to be 160
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-20_21-58-27_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts

The spec computes policy-wide modifiers (loyalty -20%, follow-up -15%) and per-item first-insurance (+10%) ALL AS PERCENTAGES OF THE BASE PREMIUM (not the running total). They are ADDITIVE adjustments.

Algorithm:
- Compute item base premium (after block discount for components)
- Item-specific modifiers ADD to total: cursed → +50% of item base; high-enchant (≥5) → +30% of item base
- First-insurance ADD: +10% of item base (per item)
- Loyalty (≥2 yr) SUBTRACT: 20% of POLICY base premium sum
- Follow-up SUBTRACT: 15% of POLICY base premium sum
- All percentages are computed on the unmodified base, then summed/subtracted
- Finally add 5 fee
- Ceiling

Verify integration example: cursed sword enchant 7, 3yr, 2nd quote:
- base 100, curse 50, ench 30, first-ins 10 → +190
- policy base = 100; loyalty -20; follow-up -15 → -35
- total = 100 + 50 + 30 + 10 - 20 - 15 = 155
- + fee 5 = 160 ✓

Also check existing tests pass:
- 2-yr loyalty + plain sword: base 100, first-ins 10, loyalty -20 = 90, +5 = 95. But test 18 expects 93.
  Wait — test expected 93. Let me recompute.
  Hmm — spec example: integration 2 says "100 base + 50 curse + 30 high-enchant - 20 loyalty + 10 first-ins - 15 follow-up = 155". So -20 = 20% of 100 (loyalty on policy base).
  Plain sword 2yr: 100 + 10 first-ins - 20 loyalty = 90; +5 = 95. But test was set to 93. Need to fix that test.

Actually the existing test 18 "long-standing customer (2 years exactly): 20% loyalty discount applies" was activated as:
```
expect(quote({ yearsWithMHPCO: 2 }, [{ type: "sword", ... }])).toBe(93);
```
Computed as 100+10=110; *0.8=88; +5=93. That was using multiplicative.

With additive: 100 + 10 - 20 = 90; +5 = 95. The test expects 93, will now fail.

Also test 17: cursed sword + amulet 3yr:
- sword: 100 + 50 + 10 = 160; amulet: 60 + 6 = 66; total items = 226
- loyalty: 20% of (100 + 60) = 32 → subtract 32
- total: 226 - 32 = 194; + 5 = 199
But test 17 expects 186. So test 17 also needs update.

So you need to FIX these existing tests in the spec file (their numeric expectations need updating to match the additive interpretation), AND update implementation.

Update test expectations to match additive interpretation:
- Test "long-standing customer (2 years exactly)": expect 95 (not 93)
- Test "item-specific modifiers apply..." (test 17): expect 199 (not 186)

Then implement additive model.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor.

content.text: I'll analyze the current state and implement the additive model.

Now I have a clear picture. The user instructed me to:
1. Update test 18 ("long-standing customer (2 years exactly)") to expect 95 (not 93)
2. Update test 17 ("item-specific modifiers apply...") to expect 199 (not 186)
3. Implement the additive model

Let me also verify other tests still pass with the additive model:
- Test 16 ("policy with cursed sword + plain amulet" newcomer): no loyalty/followup so no diff → 231 ✓
- Test 19 ("customer with 1 year"): no loyalty → 115 ✓
- Test 21 ("follow-up newcomer 2nd quote"): newcomer (no loyalty); base=100, first-ins=10, followup=-15% of 100=-15 → 100+10-15+5=100. But test expects 99. Recompute with old: 110*0.85=93.5+5=98.5→99. New: 100+10-15+5=100. So this test will need update too!

- Test 22 ("each item in follow-up"): 2 swords; base=100+100=200; first-ins=10+10=20; followup=-15%*200=-30 → 200+20-30+5=195. Test expects 192 (old: 220*0.85=187+5=192). Differs!

- Test 24 ("intermediate amounts fractional"): 3yr amulet; base=60, first-ins=6, loyalty=-12 → 60+6-12+5=59. Test expects 58. Differs!

Let me reconsider. The user explicitly says to update tests 17 and 18 only. But the additive model breaks more tests. Let me reread the user instructions carefully.

The user says: "Update test expectations to match additive interpretation: Test 'long-standing customer (2 years exactly)': expect 95 (not 93); Test 'item-specific modifiers apply...' (test 17): expect 199 (not 186)"

But by the additive logic, tests 21, 22, 24 also break. Let me recompute these carefully with the user's algorithm:

Test 21 (newcomer, followup, 1 sword): policy base = 100; sword: base 100 + first-ins 10; followup -15% of 100 = -15; total = 100+10-15+5 = 100. Test expects 99.

Test 22 (newcomer, followup, 2 swords): policy base = 200; total items = 100+10+100+10 = 220; followup = -15%*200 = -30; total = 220-30+5 = 195. Test expects 192.

Test 24 (3yr, 1 amulet): policy base = 60; amulet: 60+6=66; loyalty -20%*60 = -12; total = 66-12+5 = 59. Test expects 58.

These also need updating. Let me update all the affected tests and implementation.

content.text: Now let me implement the additive model. The "policy base premium" needs careful consideration. Looking at the user's integration example: "policy base = 100". For components, the policy base for runes would presumably be the rune block base (60) or the unit price total? Let me consider: components are part of items. I'll define policy base as the sum of item base premiums (before any item-specific modifiers and before first-insurance).

content.text:
All 26 tests pass.

🟢 Green Phase Complete:

**Implementation**: Changed policy-wide modifiers (loyalty and follow-up) from multiplicative to additive. Each is now computed as a percentage of the policy base premium sum (sum of item bases before any modifiers) and subtracted from the total. Tracked `policyBase` separately while iterating items and components.

**Result**: All 26 active tests now pass (29 todo remaining).

**Approach**: Minimal change to the algorithm — introduced a `policyBase` accumulator and switched the loyalty/follow-up steps from multiplication (`total * 0.8`, `total * 0.85`) to subtraction (`total -= policyBase * 0.2`, `total -= policyBase * 0.15`). Also updated five existing test expectations whose stated math assumed the multiplicative model: tests at lines for "long-standing customer (2 years exactly)" (95), "item-specific modifiers apply..." (199), "follow-up contract" (100), "each item in a follow-up quote" (195), and "intermediate amounts are kept as fractions" (59).

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-20_21-58-27_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-20_21-58-27_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.spec.ts

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
